---
title: "Question 1: Data Summary"
author: "Penny Hunter"
date: "2026-05-12"
output: html_document
---
Note: Run this file first 

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo    = TRUE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  fig.align = "center"
)

library(tidyverse)
library(scales)
```

## 1.0 Loading Data

```{r}
Budget_filing_2020  <- read_csv("Data/Budget_filing_2020.csv")
claim_metadata      <- read_csv("Data/claim_metadata.csv")
claims_record_2020  <- read_csv("Data/claims_record_2020.csv")
programs            <- read_csv("Data/programs.csv")
```

## 2.0 Data Inspection

```{r}
# Run for each dataset to inspect structure, summary, and missing values
str(claims_record_2020)
summary(claims_record_2020)
colSums(is.na(claims_record_2020))

str(Budget_filing_2020)
summary(Budget_filing_2020)
colSums(is.na(Budget_filing_2020))

str(programs)
summary(programs)
colSums(is.na(programs))

str(claim_metadata)
summary(claim_metadata)
colSums(is.na(claim_metadata))
```


## 3.0 Program Administrators


```{r}
# Count of claims by PA
claims_by_pa <- claims_record_2020 %>%
  count(PA, name = "Number_of_Claims") %>%
  arrange(desc(Number_of_Claims)) %>%
  mutate(Pct_Total = round(Number_of_Claims / sum(Number_of_Claims) * 100, 1))

print(claims_by_pa) 
# Figure 1: Claims by PA
bar_chart_claims_by_pa <- ggplot(
  claims_by_pa,
  aes(x = reorder(PA, Number_of_Claims), y = Number_of_Claims)
) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  geom_text(aes(label = comma(Number_of_Claims)), hjust = -0.1, size = 3) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Total Claims by Program Administrator (2020)",
    x     = "Program Administrator",
    y     = "Number of Claims"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma, expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
  theme_minimal()

print(bar_chart_claims_by_pa)
ggsave("figure1_claims_by_pa.png", plot = bar_chart_claims_by_pa,
       width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
# Supporting analysis: quarterly claim distribution — referenced in Next Steps
claims_by_quarter <- claims_record_2020 %>%
  count(`Claim Year Quarter`, name = "Number_of_Claims")

print(claims_by_quarter)

# Cross-tab by PA and quarter — confirms SCG Q4 spike
quarter_by_pa <- claims_record_2020 %>%
  count(PA, `Claim Year Quarter`, name = "Number_of_Claims") %>%
  filter(Number_of_Claims > 0) %>%
  arrange(PA, `Claim Year Quarter`)

print(quarter_by_pa)

# SCG Q4 specifically
claims_record_2020 %>%
  filter(PA == "SCG") %>%
  count(`Claim Year Quarter`, name = "Number_of_Claims") %>%
  mutate(Pct = round(Number_of_Claims / sum(Number_of_Claims) * 100, 1))
```

## 4.0 Savings by Program Group

```{r}
# Aggregate savings by Program Group (NAs excluded)
savings_by_group <- aggregate(
  cbind(`First Year Gross kWh`, `First Year Gross kW`,
        `First Year Gross Therm`, `End User Rebate`) ~ `Program Group`,
  data = claims_record_2020[!is.na(claims_record_2020$`Program Group`), ],
  FUN  = sum
)

savings_by_group$n_claims <- as.numeric(
  table(claims_record_2020$`Program Group`)
)

savings_by_group <- savings_by_group[
  order(-savings_by_group$`First Year Gross kWh`), 
]

print(savings_by_group)

# Top 10 for chart
savings_top10 <- savings_by_group %>%
  arrange(desc(`First Year Gross kWh`)) %>%
  head(10)
```

```{r}
# missing Program Group by PA — supports footnote in report
claims_record_2020 %>%
  filter(PA == "SCG") %>%
  summarise(
    total         = n(),
    has_group     = sum(!is.na(`Program Group`)),
    pct_has_group = round(mean(!is.na(`Program Group`)) * 100, 1)
  )

claims_record_2020 %>%
  group_by(PA) %>%
  summarise(
    total_claims       = n(),
    pct_missing_group  = round(mean(is.na(`Program Group`)) * 100, 1)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(pct_missing_group))
```

```{r}
# Figure 2: Savings by Program Group
program_group_savings <- ggplot(
  savings_top10,
  aes(x = reorder(`Program Group`, `First Year Gross kWh`),
      y = `First Year Gross kWh`)
) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = comma(round(`First Year Gross kWh`, 0))),
    hjust = -0.1, size = 3
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title   = "Top 10 Program Groups by Electric Savings (2020)",
    x       = "Program Group",
    y       = "First Year Gross kWh",
    caption = "Top 10 of 29 program groups shown. ~78% of claims missing Program Group label excluded."
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma, expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.2))) +
  theme_minimal()

print(program_group_savings)
ggsave("figure2_savings_by_program_group.png", plot = program_group_savings,
       width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```

## 5.0 Customer Segments

```{r}
# residential vs commercial split — 93% figure cited in report
claims_record_2020 %>%
  count(`Measure Residential Flag`, name = "Number_of_Claims") %>%
  mutate(Pct = round(Number_of_Claims / sum(Number_of_Claims) * 100, 1))
```

```{r}
# Prepare building type data for pie chart
building_pie <- claims_record_2020 %>%
  count(`Building Type`, name = "Number_of_Claims") %>%
  arrange(desc(Number_of_Claims)) %>%
  mutate(
    Building_Type = ifelse(Number_of_Claims < 10000, "Other", `Building Type`)
  ) %>%
  group_by(Building_Type) %>%
  summarise(Number_of_Claims = sum(Number_of_Claims)) %>%
  mutate(Pct = round(Number_of_Claims / sum(Number_of_Claims) * 100, 1))

print(building_pie)
```

```{r}
# Figure 3: Claims by building type
pie_chart <- ggplot(
  building_pie,
  aes(x = "", y = Number_of_Claims, fill = Building_Type)
) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
  coord_polar("y") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(Pct, "%")),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 3
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Residential Single Family" = "steelblue",
    "Residential Mobile Home"   = "gold",
    "Residential Multi-family"  = "darkgreen",
    "Commercial"                = "salmon",
    "Other"                     = "purple"
  )) +
  labs(
    title   = "Claims by Building Type (2020)",
    fill    = "Building Type",
    caption = "Categories with fewer than 10,000 claims grouped into 'Other'"
  ) +
  theme_void()

print(pie_chart)
ggsave("figure3_building_type.png", plot = pie_chart,
       width = 7, height = 7, dpi = 300)
```
